title: KPI filters issue from 2 boards in 2 separate workspaces

posts.author: Tevans (Tom Evans)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,

I’m trying to create a KPI dashboard that pulls through data from multiple boards nested in multiple boards, but I’m having difficulty setting up the required filters I need.

For example, I have BOARD A in Workspace 1. I also have BOARD B and BOARD C in workspace 2. In my KPI dashboard when I only connect BOARD A, I can see BOARD A filter parameters (ie the columns on that board).

However, when I then connect BOARD B and BOARD C I can only see column parameters from BOARD B and C, and can no longer see column paramters from BOARD A even though it is still connected.

What I need to be able to do that effectively does this:
Where COLUMN (People - Board A) is assigned to xxx
AND Where COLUMN (People - Board B and C) is assigned to xxx

But having all three boards connected, I’m unable to see the People column that is on BOARD A. When I disconnect BOARD B and C from the dashboard, I can see the People column for BOARD A.

What am I overlooking?

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Tevans ,

It could be a number of things, but usually I find the issue to be the naming convention of the associated columns. If they aren’t aligned, Monday won’t necessarily group them together.

Before reading further, ensure that you have access to all 3 boards. Next, ensure that your entire dashboard isn’t filtered to see specific information from a specific board. Next, make sure that your chart is able to see every board (go to the chart Settings –> Boards, make sure all relevant boards are selected).

If you made sure that all the above is in order, and you still don’t see the data you need, then you will need to do one of two things:

Option 1:

  1. Go to the Settings of the chart.
  2. Click on the X-axis area
  3. Hit Manage

Screenshot 2025-09-07 at 10.43.26334×430 15.1 KB

  1. Find your Person column and make sure to Customize so all 3 boards have matching Person columns.

Screenshot 2025-09-07 at 10.45.22296×268 12.4 KB

Option 2:

  1. Go to the Settings of the chart.
  2. Click on the X-axis area
  3. Select “One by one”
  4. Make sure to select the correct Person column for each board.

Screenshot 2025-09-07 at 10.47.03310×355 12.6 KB

posts.author: Tevans (Tom Evans)
posts.cookedHtml:
DavidSchenkler:

ttings of the char

Hey @DavidSchenkler

Thanks for the reply! I’m unsure why I couldn’t see both columns, but the issue is now fixed - I renamed them so they matched and that allowed me to filter both columns from the boards.

Thanks!

posts.author: james598patton (James Patton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Tevans:

Hey,

I’m trying to create a KPI dashboard that pulls through data from multiple boards nested in multiple boards, but I’m having difficulty setting up the required filters I need.

For example, I have BOARD A in Workspace 1. I also have BOARD B and BOARD C in workspace 2. In my KPI dashboard when I only connect BOARD A, I can see BOARD A filter parameters (ie the columns on that board).

However, when I then connect BOARD B and BOARD C I can only see column parameters from BOARD B and C, and can no longer see column paramters from BOARD A even though it is still connected.

What I need to be able to do that effectively does this:
Where COLUMN (People - Board A) is assigned to xxx
AND Where COLUMN (People - Board B and C) is assigned to xxx

But having all three boards connected, I’m unable to see the People column that is on BOARD A. When I disconnect BOARD B and C from the dashboard, I can see the People column for BOARD A.

What am I overlooking?

Hello @Tevans,
This issue likely stems from aessuccess login column name conflicts or schema mismatches across workspaces. KPI dashboards often prioritize shared schema, so when BOARD B and C override BOARD A’s column structure, its filters may be hidden. To fix this, standardize column names (e.g., “People”) across all boards and ensure they’re mapped consistently. Alternatively, use separate widgets per board to preserve individual filters.

Best Regards,
James Patton
